_note: MOCK procedure result for PT-041. No real patient. For PreChart/LeftBehind development/eval.

procedure.name: Colonoscopy with biopsy
procedure.indication: Iron-deficiency anemia (Hgb 9.1, MCV 76, ferritin 11) with positive fecal occult blood and unintentional weight loss (~10 lb/3 mo) in an 88-year-old — evaluation for occult GI blood loss/malignancy. Proceeded after documented goals-of-care/shared decision-making with POA daughter, cardiology, and PCP.
procedure.sedation: Monitored anesthesia care (anesthesia-administered propofol), titrated for frailty and advanced age; hemodynamically stable throughout.
procedure.prep_quality: Boston Bowel Prep Scale 6/9 (right 1, transverse 2, left 3) — adequate but suboptimal in the right colon; mucosa examined after additional washing/suctioning.
procedure.extent_reached: Cecum (confirmed by appendiceal orifice and ileocecal valve); terminal ileum intubated.

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: Colonoscopy with cold-forceps biopsies. INDICATION: iron-deficiency anemia with positive FOBT and weight loss in an 88-year-old; occult GI blood loss/malignancy workup. CONSENT: informed consent obtained from healthcare POA (daughter) as surrogate given patient's moderate dementia; capacity for independent consent not established. ANTICOAGULATION: apixaban (patient confirmed on 5 mg PO BID per pharmacy bottle; chart had listed 2.5 mg BID — reconciled) held for 48 hours pre-procedure in coordination with cardiology; last dose morning of 2026-08-04. SEDATION: monitored anesthesia care (propofol). PREP: adequate but suboptimal in the right colon (BBPS 6). FINDINGS: The colonoscope was advanced to the cecum with terminal ileum intubation. In the ascending colon a partially circumferential, fungating, friable mass approximately 4 cm was identified, occupying roughly one-third to one-half of the luminal circumference, non-obstructing, with contact bleeding on gentle manipulation and no active spontaneous bleeding. The lumen remained traversable. Multiple cold-forceps biopsies (6) were obtained from the mass margin and sent to pathology (specimen A); the site was tattooed with sterile carbon ink distally for future localization. A separate 6 mm sessile polyp was noted in the sigmoid colon and removed by cold snare polypectomy (specimen B). Scattered non-bleeding diverticula were present in the sigmoid. The terminal ileum and the remainder of the examined colon were otherwise unremarkable. IMPRESSION: Fungating ~4 cm mass in the ascending colon, suspicious for malignancy — biopsied and tattooed, pathology pending; likely source of the iron-deficiency anemia and occult blood loss. 6 mm sigmoid polyp removed. Diverticulosis. PLAN: Expedite pathology; staging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis and CEA; multidisciplinary review with colorectal surgery and oncology in the context of goals of care and the patient's frailty/dementia; communicate results with POA.

findings.finding: Fungating partially circumferential friable mass, ~4 cm, non-obstructing, contact bleeding — suspicious for malignancy
findings.action_taken: 6 cold-forceps biopsies obtained; site tattooed distally

findings.finding: Diverticulosis, no stigmata of recent bleeding

disposition: Recovered from anesthesia without incident; discharged same day to assisted living with daughter/POA escort. Post-procedure and post-polypectomy precautions reviewed with daughter. Given the mass findings, results to be discussed with POA once pathology returns.

followup.recommendation: Expedite and communicate ascending colon mass biopsy pathology to the POA; if malignant, coordinate multidisciplinary care in context of goals of care
Obtain staging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis and CEA pending pathology; discuss with colorectal surgery and oncology
Reconvene goals-of-care/shared decision-making with POA, cardiology, and PCP to determine extent of oncologic workup and treatment appropriate to frailty/dementia
Resume apixaban 48 hours post-procedure if no bleeding, and reconcile intended dose (5 mg vs 2.5 mg BID) with cardiology given low body weight ~47 kg and age >=80
Review sigmoid polyp pathology; continue iron-deficiency anemia management/iron repletion independent of oncologic decisions

planted_followup_items.description: Ascending colon fungating ~4 cm mass suspicious for malignancy identified on colonoscopy — the likely source of the iron-deficiency anemia and occult bleeding; must not be dropped.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: Ensure biopsy result is tracked and communicated to POA; escalate to multidisciplinary colorectal surgery/oncology review

planted_followup_items.description: Ascending colon mass biopsy pathology (specimen A) pending — result determines diagnosis and drives staging/treatment decisions.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: Track pathology, communicate result to POA, initiate staging if malignant

planted_followup_items.description: Referral to colorectal surgery and oncology, plus staging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis and CEA, contingent on pathology and goals of care.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: Place referrals and staging orders once pathology confirms; frame within frailty/dementia goals of care

planted_followup_items.description: Apixaban held 48h peri-procedurally (patient actually on 5 mg BID; chart listed 2.5 mg BID). Resumption after biopsy/polypectomy plus unresolved dose-reconciliation with cardiology (low weight ~47 kg, age >=80 favor reduced dose).
planted_followup_items.expected_action: Confirm safe resumption timing and reconcile intended apixaban dose with cardiology

planted_followup_items.description: Sigmoid polyp pathology (specimen B) pending — determines polyp histology and any surveillance implications.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: Review polyp histology; document any surveillance interval (subordinate to oncologic plan)

planted_followup_items.description: Iron-deficiency anemia (Hgb 9.1) with a friable contact-bleeding mass in a patient on full-dose DOAC — ongoing anemia/blood-loss risk requires Hgb monitoring and iron repletion independent of oncologic decisions.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: Monitor Hgb, arrange iron repletion (oral vs IV), set transfusion threshold, weigh anticoagulation net benefit

planted_followup_items.description: Suboptimal right-colon prep (BBPS right segment 1) in the setting of a right-sided mass — if pathology is non-diagnostic or lesion incompletely characterized, consider repeat/cross-sectional imaging.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: Ensure mass adequately characterized; consider repeat evaluation only if clinically warranted and consistent with goals of care

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Contacts are relayed by the daughter/POA given the patient's moderate dementia. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored.

contacts.verbatim: Mom's a little groggy today and says her throat feels scratchy, but she ate some breakfast at the home.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected residual post-anesthesia grogginess and mild post-endoscopy sore throat; tolerating intake
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; grogginess and sore throat are expected after sedation/scope and self-limited within 24-48h. Ensure hydration; call for fever, severe abdominal pain, or bleeding.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy/sedation symptoms in a frail elderly patient; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: The assisted living nurse is asking when we should give Mom her apixaban again, and when we'll hear back about the biopsy from the growth you found.
contacts.clinical_signal: POA asking about anticoagulation resumption timing and pending mass pathology
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the plan: resume apixaban ~48h post-procedure if no bleeding, with dose to be reconciled with cardiology (5 mg vs 2.5 mg BID given low weight/age); pathology pending (~day 5), team will call POA with the result and next steps.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the follow-up plan; links to the med_resume item (F4) and touches pending pathology; safety-relevant but not urgent as long as no bleeding.

contacts.verbatim: There was a little pink streak on the toilet paper once after her bowel movement this morning. She feels fine, no dizziness, and it hasn't happened again.
contacts.clinical_signal: Minor self-limited spotting after cold-snare polypectomy of a small (6 mm) polyp; hemodynamically well, single episode
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; a small amount of pink/streaky blood once after a cold-snare polypectomy is common and self-limited. Give return precautions: call or seek care for repeated or large-volume bleeding, clots, dizziness, black stools, or lightheadedness — especially once apixaban is resumed.
contacts.rationale: Dangerous-sounding-but-benign: minor one-time spotting after a small cold-snare polypectomy in a stable patient does not warrant escalation, but return precautions matter given resumed anticoagulation.

contacts.verbatim: It's been almost two weeks and nobody has called us about Mom's biopsy from the growth. Should we be worried? What happens next?
contacts.clinical_signal: POA following up on the pending mass pathology (expected back ~day 5); results should now be available and communicated
contacts.expected_action: Retrieve the specimen A pathology (expected back 2026-08-11) and schedule a call/visit with the POA to convey the result and next steps (staging, colorectal surgery/oncology review framed within goals of care). Ensure the result was not dropped.
contacts.rationale: Loop-closure on the critical pending pathology; links to F2. Not a clinical emergency, but flags a possible dropped follow-up on a malignancy-suspicious result that must be actively closed.
